diseases.name: Powdery Mildew
diseases.impact: Initially appears as light yellow spots on mature leaves. These spots enlarge as white, fluffy mycelium grows on the leaf surface. Severely affected leaves turn brown, shrivel, and drop.
diseases.solution: Use recommended fungicides at the early stage of disease development. Remove infected crop residues after harvest.

diseases.name: Downy Mildew
diseases.impact: Angular yellow spots delimited by leaf veins on the upper surface of leaves. Leaves become chlorotic and eventually necrotic, leading to host plant cell death.
diseases.solution: Use recommended fungicides at the early stage of disease development. Remove infected plant parts after harvest.

diseases.name: Foot Rot / Basal Rot
diseases.impact: In early stages, plants show temporary wilting symptoms. Leaves exhibit yellowing, wilting, and drooping. Eventually, the plant dies. Vascular bundles in the basal region turn yellow or brown. A white fungal network is visible at the base of the plant.
diseases.solution: Destroy infected plants along with the soil. Improve drainage. Use recommended fungicides at the early stage of disease development.

diseases.name: Gummy Stem Blight
diseases.impact: Lesions typically begin as water-soaked areas on leaves and fruits. Early stages may show chlorotic holes, which then turn light brown and become irregularly shaped. Dark, cracked, sunken spots appear on fruits. Plants may collapse at sunken, bark-cankered areas on the stem. Stem lesions often elongate like streaks, and gum exudation may occur from cracks. Dark brown to black pycnidia can be seen on fruits, stems, or leaves.
diseases.solution: Deep plow crop residues immediately after harvest to reduce fungal survival. Use recommended fungicides at the early stage of disease development.

diseases.name: Bacterial Wilt
diseases.impact: Sudden wilting of leaves is observed. Eventually, the entire plant wilts. Leaves turn brown and dry. Vascular discoloration (browning) occurs, leading to permanent wilting.
diseases.solution: Destroy infected plants along with the soil. Practice crop rotation with resistant varieties (okra, corn). Implement mixed cropping with Cruciferae family crops. Avoid transferring equipment from infected fields to uninfected fields.

diseases.name: Leaf Curl Virus (LCV)
diseases.impact: Upward curling of leaves, shortened internodes, and deformation. Stunted plants and deformed fruits.
diseases.solution: Control of the whitefly vector (Bemicia tabaci) is essential.

diseases.name: Papaya Ringspot Virus (PRSV)
diseases.impact: Variegated spotted lesions on leaves. Leaf deformation. Fruit deformation and reduction in fruit size. In severe infections, oily spots appear on the fruit surface.
diseases.solution: Control of aphid vectors is essential.

diseases.name: Cucumber Mosaic Virus (CMV)
diseases.impact: Green spots and leaf deformation. Green spots on fruits, which turn bright yellow and decay. Fruit deformation.

diseases.name: Zucchini Yellow Mosaic Virus (ZYMV)
diseases.impact: Stunted plants, yellowing, chlorosis (occasionally), variegated and deformed leaves (blisters). Fruit discoloration and deformation.

diseases.name: Phytoplasma Disease / Little Leaf Disease
diseases.impact: Small, thick, and yellow-green leaves. Shortened internodes and stunted plants. Chrysanthemum-like appearance. Greening of flowers.
diseases.solution: Remove infected plants. Control vectors (leafhoppers) using recommended insecticides.
